description: thrive-social post queue. Refilled locally with the Claude skill; drained by post-from-queue.js (GitHub Actions or local). Entries publish only when approved=true, oldest first. Append new entries; never edit posted ones.

posts.caption:
Same salary. Same city. Wildly different futures.

James got a $300/month raise and upgraded his car.
David got the same raise and automated it into an index fund.

20 years later, at a 7% average annual return:
James has a 12-year-old car.
David has about $156,000.

The raise was never the difference.
The system was.

(Hypothetical illustration. Historical average return — not a guarantee.)

#moneymindset #personalfinance #wealthbuilding

scenes.mediaSource: Two similar suburban houses side by side at dusk, warm window lights, cinematic, photorealistic, no text in image
scenes.script: Same salary. Same city. Wildly different futures. Meet James and David.

scenes.mediaSource: Split composition: a shiny new car on a driveway on one side, a phone showing an investing app with an automatic transfer on the other, photorealistic, no text in image
scenes.script: James got a three hundred dollar a month raise and upgraded his car. David automated the same raise into a boring index fund.

scenes.mediaSource: Sunrise over a quiet suburban street, hopeful golden light, cinematic, photorealistic, no text in image
scenes.script: Twenty years later, at a seven percent average annual return, David has about one hundred and fifty six thousand dollars. James has a twelve year old car.

scenes.mediaSource: A person walking confidently along a tree-lined street at dawn, back to camera, golden hour, cinematic, photorealistic, no text in image
scenes.script: The raise was never the difference. The system was.

posts.caption:
7 money rules that quietly build wealth:

1. Pay yourself first — automatically.
2. Never finance a lifestyle. Only assets.
3. If you can't buy it twice, you can't afford it.
4. Invest before you feel ready. Time beats timing.
5. Keep 3–6 months of expenses boring and liquid.
6. Every raise: half to the future, half to today.
7. Track your net worth, not your income.

Screenshot this. Rule 4 pays for the rest.

#personalfinance #moneyrules #wealthbuilding

scenes.mediaSource: Minimalist desk with an open notebook, pen and morning coffee, soft window light, photorealistic, no text in image
scenes.script: Seven money rules that quietly build wealth.

scenes.mediaSource: Close-up of hands setting up an automatic transfer on a phone banking app, warm light, photorealistic, no text in image
scenes.script: Pay yourself first, automatically. Never finance a lifestyle, only assets. And if you can't buy it twice, you can't afford it.

scenes.mediaSource: A calm person drinking coffee by a window at sunrise, peaceful confident mood, photorealistic, no text in image
scenes.script: Invest before you feel ready, because time beats timing. Keep three to six months of expenses liquid. And split every raise: half to the future, half to today.

scenes.mediaSource: Wide shot of a city skyline at dawn from a calm rooftop, hopeful mood, cinematic, photorealistic, no text in image
scenes.script: Finally, track your net worth, not your income. That's the scoreboard that actually matters.

posts.caption:
You don't rise to your income. You fall to your system.

A bigger salary with no system disappears.
A modest salary with a system compounds.

That's why two people earning the same money can end up decades apart.

If you want the exact system — step by step, starting from zero — it's all in The Wealth Masterclass. Link below.

#personalfinance #wealthbuilding
